sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to urge, to press any one on (comp. Ch. אֲצַץ. Cognate roots, both in sound and in signification, are אָלַץ, לָחַץ, נָחַץ; comp. Gr. πιέζω), Exo 5:13.

(2) to urge oneself, to hasten, Jos 10:13 Pro 19:2 28:20 . Followed by מִן it is, to hasten backward, to withdraw oneself; Jer 17:16, מֵרֹעֶה אַחֲרֶיךָ לֹא אַצְתִּי for מִהְיֹות רֹעֶה “I have not withdrawn myself, that I should not be a shepherd (prophet) after thee.”

(3) to be narrow, strait, Jos 17:15.

Hiphil, i.q. Kal No. 1, to urge, to press on; construed with a gerund of the verb, Isa 22:4 followed by בְּ of pers., Gen 19:15.
